nuggets.headline: For 'In Rainbows,' Radiohead's sonic warmth comes not just from their performances, but from a classic piece of analog gear: the EMT 140 plate reverb.
nuggets.text: Producer Nigel Godrich is known for his love of vintage equipment, and the EMT 140 plate reverb is a key component of his sound. This massive piece of gear, literally a large metal plate suspended in a wooden box, imparts a natural and spacious reverberation that digital plugins struggle to replicate. On 'In Rainbows,' this reverb is used to create a sense of depth and warmth, particularly on Thom Yorke's vocals and Phil Selway's drums, contributing to the album's intimate and organic feel.

source.title: Deep Cuts: The Making of OK Computer - Audio Media International
source.quoteSnippet: To further expand the spacious ambience, Radiohead’s producer brought along his EMT 140 Plate Reverb.

nuggets.headline: The hypnotic, tumbling quality of "Weird Fishes/Arpeggi" is achieved through a rhythmic illusion: the guitars are playing in a different time signature than the drums.
nuggets.text: Phil Selway's drumming provides a steady, unambiguous 4/4 pulse throughout the song. Against this, the interlocking guitar arpeggios of Jonny Greenwood and Ed O'Brien are played in groups of three eighth notes, creating a 3-against-4 polyrhythm. This rhythmic dissonance is what gives the track its signature feeling of perpetual motion and unresolved tension, as the guitar patterns continually shift their alignment with the underlying beat.

source.title: S11E5 - Weird Fishes / Arpeggi by Radiohead Transcript - Dissect
source.quoteSnippet: But because the guitar is playing in groups of three and the drums are playing in groups of four. We get a three against four grouping, dissonant.

nuggets.headline: If the grand, layered soundscapes of 'In Rainbows' resonate with you, you owe it to yourself to explore Spiritualized's 1997 masterpiece, 'Ladies and Gentlemen We Are Floating in Space'.
nuggets.text: While sonically distinct, both albums share a commitment to creating immersive, emotionally resonant worlds through lush instrumentation and ambitious production. Spiritualized, led by Jason Pierce, took a 'more is more' approach, employing a gospel choir and orchestra to build a wall of sound that is simultaneously intimate and epic. For a taste of this, start with the title track and then move on to "Come Together" to hear a different, yet equally compelling, take on creating a grand sonic tapestry.

source.title: Ladies and Gentlemen We Are Floating in Space - Wikipedia
source.quoteSnippet: The album features guest appearances from the Balanescu Quartet, the London Community Gospel Choir and Dr. John.

nuggets.imageCaption: Spiritualized 'Ladies and Gentlemen We Are Floating in Space' — layered sonic grandeur
